name: Millard County
description_short: Millard County Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving the community since 1956, offering savings accounts, loans, credit cards, and mobile banking.
description_long:
Millard County Credit Union (MCCU) has been operating as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative since 1956, building a foundation of community trust over seven decades. The institution serves thousands of members with approximately $M in assets and operates multiple branches including locations in Delta and other service areas. As a credit union, MCCU is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and operates under the cooperative model where members are owners rather than customers of a corporation.

MCCU provides a comprehensive suite of retail financial services including share savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts (IRAs), auto loans, mortgages, personal consolidation loans, and credit cards with no annual fees. Members gain access to 24/7 mobile banking through their app, which supports mobile deposits, bill payments, electronic statements, and digital wallet functionality. The institution emphasizes competitive rates, with offerings including savings accounts earning up to 0.050% APY, money market accounts at 1.250% APY, certificates reaching 3.919% APY, mortgages as low as 4.875% APR, and auto loans starting at 4.99% APR.

MCCU distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure, which theoretically allows earnings to be returned to members rather than shareholders, and its emphasis on member education and fraud awareness. The institution provides educational content on online security, fraud protection tips, identity theft prevention, and fraud alerts. The credit union holds annual membership meetings (next scheduled for April 22, 2026) with board election processes and voting opportunities, enabling democratic member governance. Their focus on accessibility includes both in-person branch banking and comprehensive digital services.

As a regional credit union, MCCU's membership eligibility may be restricted to residents of Millard County or surrounding areas, which could limit accessibility for consumers outside their geographic footprint. While they offer competitive rates on savings and loans, prospective members should verify membership requirements before applying. The institution appears well-managed and community-focused, though as a smaller regional credit union, they may have more limited product options compared to larger national credit unions or banks.
services: Credit cards with no annual fees
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit
pros: No annual fees on credit cards
Competitive certificate rates up to 3.919% APY
24/7 mobile banking app with mobile deposit capability
Member-owned not-for-profit structure allows earnings to return to members
Strong fraud awareness and online security education programs
Democratic governance with annual member meetings and board elections
Serving the same community for 70 years (established 1956)
cons: Membership likely restricted to Millard County residents or defined service area, limiting geographic accessibility
Smaller regional institution may offer fewer product varieties than national credit unions
Website does not clearly specify all membership eligibility requirements upfront
best_for: Millard County residents seeking member-owned financial services with competitive rates
Consumers prioritizing fraud protection education and community banking relationships
Members wanting no-fee credit cards and mobile-first banking convenience
Local savers and borrowers seeking democratic governance in their financial institution
diagnosis: Millard County Credit Union is best for residents within its membership area seeking community-based financial services with competitive rates and strong member governance. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is likely geographically restricted, making this institution inaccessible to consumers outside Millard County.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
